assertion_results.text: The response describes locating the digest comment using the marker string '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' from shared/description-digest-protocol.md
assertion_results.evidence: digest-match.md line 19: "Search all returned comments for those whose body starts with the marker string `[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:`." This exactly matches the required marker string from the protocol.

assertion_results.text: The response describes computing a digest of the current description (using scripts/sha256-digest.py or SHA-256) and comparing it with the stored digest
assertion_results.evidence: digest-match.md lines 44-48: describes extracting the description, writing to a temp file, and running "python3 scripts/sha256-digest.py /tmp/desc-TC-9201.txt". Lines 62-67 show explicit comparison of stored hex vs computed hex digests, both identified as SHA-256 tagged format (sha256-md).

assertion_results.text: The response confirms the digests match and states it will proceed silently without prompting the user — no additional latency for the happy path
assertion_results.evidence: digest-match.md line 68: "Result: MATCH." Lines 72-75: "The digests match. Per the SKILL.md specification (Step 1.5, item 4e): Match: proceed silently -- no additional user prompt, no added latency."

assertion_results.text: The response does not alert the user or pause execution due to the digest check — it proceeds directly to subsequent steps
assertion_results.evidence: digest-match.md line 76: "The skill proceeds directly to Step 2 (Verify Dependencies) without any user interaction, warnings, or prompts." plan.md line 53: "Proceed silently -- no user prompt needed." The plan then immediately proceeds to Step 2 (line 58) with no intervening alerts or pauses.
